lines.text: Don't cut me, punch me, just let me go
Into the nurse's office where I float away
I'm pale as the loose-leaf paper they grow
From hollowing out all my lungs in the snow
Yeah, I'm coughing, I'm bleeding
Band-Aids won't heal it
'Cause they hate me, so I'm fakin'
All of this so they take-take me
Take me home, home, home
Give me that pink slip of permission
This is old, old, old
I'm tired of wishing I was ditching
Ooh, ooh, ooh-ooh
Ooh, ooh-ooh
Teacher, can I sit right there?
This bitch behind me is cutting my hair
"No, just sit your ass down at the chalkboard, you stare"
I faked up a seizure and left then and there
Yeah, I'm coughing, I'm bleeding
Band-Aids won't heal it
'Cause they hate me, so I'm fakin'
All of this so they take-take me
Take me home, home, home
Give me that pink slip of permission
This is old, old, old
I'm tired of wishing I was ditching
Ooh, ooh, ooh-ooh
Ooh, ooh-ooh
Take me home, home, home
Give me that pink slip of permission
This is old, old, old
I'm tired of wishing I was ditching
Home, home, home
Old, old, old

children: It’s nothing new, she has been bullied and hurt since she was first born, by her own mother. Almost everyone she came into contact during this lifetime has tried or ended up causing her harm. She is tired of such and just wants to move on. She doesn’t wants to face such trials anymore.

Crybaby is coughing and bleeding due to the harm she caused to herself in order to have a motive to be out of the ambient where she is bullied and harmed, however she adds such information by saying that no band-aids or no medical attention can actually heal her, because the classmates despise her, and will continue to keep hurting her as much as they can, leaving her with no option but to keep faking a play where she is too sick to go to school.

31935572.fragment:
Yeah, I’m coughing
 I’m bleeding, Band-Aids won’t heal it
 ‘Cause they hate me, so I’m fakin’
 All of, of this so they take-take me

children: Loose leaf paper is essentially any paper that is not bound in place/together. Of course paper is very white and could be described as pale just like Melanie. She is saying how pale she really is.
By hollowing out her lungs in the snow she could be throwing up or coughing violently (maybe to the point of coughing up blood) which could make her lightheaded and pale.

30061533.fragment:
I’m pale as the loose-leaf paper they grow
 From hollowing out all my lungs in the snow

children: “Ditching” is playing off of the theme of school for the name of the album,
, where students intentionally skip class. Cry Baby has endured so much bullying that she wants to avoid going to school. Another
definition
of “ditching” refers to digging a trench in the ground. She wants to fight off the thoughts of throwing herself in a ditch and wishing she was dead.

19389060.fragment: I’m tired of wishing I was ditching

children: In this lyric, Crybaby is pleading to not be bullied or bothered anymore and to just be left alone in the “Nurse’s Office” where she can escape the antagonists of the movie
(Kelly & her minions, teachers, the principal)
and the suffering they put her through.
This is a common problem in many modern schools, with kids being bullied to the point that getting sent to the nurses office is the only place they can escape the torment.

Melanie elaborates on this throughout the song, describing how sick she is of having to get a “pink slip of permission” just to escape relentless bullying, something that shouldn’t be happening in the first place.
https://youtu.be/2HtaIvb61Uk?t=1716

17979285.fragment:
Don’t cut me, punch me, just let me go
 Into the nurse’s office where I float away

children: During the
film, Crybaby is bullied by Kelly and her gang, who taunt her for her looks and different personality.

In this lyric, Crybaby asks to move seats to avoid the bullying, but the teacher ignores her emotional pain and prioritises the seemingly unimportant lesson. Martinez therefore reveals how school systems treat kids poorly, by ignoring when they need real help and only doing their basic job. They teach every child in an identical manner and refuse to acknowledge individual needs or provide genuine help.
In response to the teacher’s dismissal, Cry Baby “faked up a seizure” to leave the classroom and avoid the bullying.

17979242.fragment:
Teacher, can I sit right there?
 This bitch behind me is cutting my hair
 “No, just sit your ass down at the chalkboard, you stare”
 I faked up a seizure and left then and there

children: Cry Baby doesn’t cope well with being bullied. In the
film
, she is teased, called cruel names, and even has her hair cut by peers. In order to escape this, she “fakes” illness, something children will
often do to get out of school or other activities.

Interpreted literally, this line acknowledges how children faking illness promise “band-aids won’t heal it” so that they have an excuse not to go back to class. But it also notes that physical assistance doesn’t cure emotional pain.
This lyric draws a parallel to 2015’s
“Training Wheels”
, where Cry Baby needed Band-Aids to protect her from the perils of love:
I carry Band-Aids on me now
For when your soft hands hit the jagged ground

17978286.fragment:
Yeah, I’m coughing
 I’m bleeding, Band-Aids won’t heal it
 ‘Cause they hate me, so I’m fakin’
 All of, of this so they take-take me

children: Usually, by saying pink slip, we mean a notice of dismissal from employment. But in this case Cry Baby uses it as a permission slip for school, usually, those slips are white. She wants to take the slip of permission to leave the school ground.
But in other Schools, Pink Slips normally mean girls are allowed to go to the toilet because of a period which could reference to her other song on the album, “Strawberry Shortcake.”

17426980.fragment: Give me that pink slip of permission
